WTI.data_anomaly: price_source_conflict: fast_info prev_close $100.53 vs history $95.08 diverge by 5.5%. Signal suppressed.

macro_summary: The S&P 500 climbed 1.02% to a fresh record 733.83 on Wednesday as Iran-Hormuz de-escalation and AMD's blowout Q1 earnings ignited a broad rally. WTI settled at $95.72 (source conflict noted — fast_info prev close $100.53 vs history $95.08), easing inflation fears ahead of today's FOMC decision. The VIX slipped to 17.39, and the US10Y fell 5 basis points to 4.36%, reinforcing risk-on sentiment.
commodities_note: WTI data shows a price source conflict — the intraday snapshot flagged a 5.5% divergence between fast_info ($100.53) and history ($95.08); the official settlement near $95.72 reflects de-escalation. Gold rose 1.12% to $4,705.50 as real yields dipped. The US10Y declined 5 bp to 4.36%, moving closer to the 4.20% NIM-reset threshold for Singapore banks.

singapore.summary: Singapore banks await UOB (today) and OCBC (tomorrow) earnings. NIM compression headwind persists with US10Y at 4.36%, still above the 4.20% threshold. OCBC's HSBC Indonesia acquisition (per Private Banker International, May 5) adds wealth scale but doesn't solve the margin problem.
singapore.key_development: UOB reports today; OCBC reports tomorrow — both prints are critical to gauge whether NIMs are stabilising below 4.20% US10Y.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX surged 2.39% to a record 506.87, but the rally has driven 7 of 11 watchlist members into hard-blocked CAUTION territory on extreme extension. AMD's earnings beat and NVDA's Corning deal drove momentum, yet no ticker offers a clean entry window. TSM is the sole on_deck candidate with PEG 1.25 and 35% revenue growth, but it's still 16% above its 50-day.
semiconductors.key_development: AMD's Q1 beat and NVDA's $500M Corning deal extended semis to levels where every entry requires a 10-20% pullback; CoWoS capacity remains the structural bottleneck.

big_tech.summary: BigTech cluster is split: MSFT holds near its 50-day without a block, while AMZN and GOOG are CAUTION at extreme extension. PLTR's 66x P/E keeps it on the sidelines. The AI capex story is validated, but price has run too far ahead of near-term entry triggers.
big_tech.thesis_status: Bullish capex trajectory, but valuation gaps widening
big_tech.key_development: MSFT is the only BigTech name without an extension block; the others need a 15-25% correction before entry windows reopen.

interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's earnings composure pulls COHR down 8-10% from current levels.

interconnected.reason: NVDA $500M AI infrastructure deal — fiber optics plants supply data center interconnects; benefits from AI capex cycle.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Corning pulls back 5-7% and NVDA holds above $195.

interconnected.name: Arm Holdings
interconnected.reason: Earnings mover late; AI chip design licensing — correlated with semis rally; watch for read-through on NVDA's Blackwell ramp.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after ARM's next earnings for confirmation of AI licensing revenue acceleration above 30%.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz oil crisis de-escalating: Trump announces pause, diplomatic push; WTI down from $105 spike to $95.72, but supply risk persists.
MATCH Act export controls: proposed US legislation would restrict ASML DUV/EUV exports to China — pending committee action per Simply Wall St, May 7.
US-China chip tariffs: new 0% tariffs on Chinese chips effective 2027, on top of existing 50% (Tom's Hardware, May 7); near-term impact limited.
Fed rate pause: FOMC decision today; no change expected, but hawkish rhetoric could delay semis recovery.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil easing from Hormuz de-escalation lifted broad indices to new highs. Singapore banks (DBS, OCBC, UOB) remain under NIM pressure as US10Y stays above 4.20%. Semis surged but are universally extended — entry windows are closed across NVDA, AMD, MU, and ASML.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump administration announces Hormuz pause and new chip tariffs (delayed to 2027), per Tom's Hardware; WTI settled lower despite source conflict.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries into extended semis; wait for post-FOMC pullbacks to rebuild support-tested positions in TSM (on_deck) and MSFT.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — no entries, watch for pullbacks
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Eighteen of 21 names are hard-blocked on extreme extension; the only three holds are awaiting earnings. The semis rally has fully priced near-term catalysts, leaving no entry windows. FOMC today and multiple earnings this week keep risk elevated.
